title: rosa canina subsp. suffulta
intro: Rosa canina subsp. suffulta, also known as the dwarf dog rose or downy dog rose, is a subspecies of the dog rose that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina subsp. suffulta include its high content of vitamin C, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, making it a popular natural remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues and skin problems. Rosa canina subsp. suffulta can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in the production of herbal extracts and oils.
